message:
test(cli,core): isolate STORAGE_ROOT per-test and serialize process-env overrides

Background
----------
Many CLI-layer tests in `crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/lib.rs` seed
their own temp SQLite DBs and archive mailbox layouts, but they
used to inherit the real `STORAGE_ROOT` from the surrounding
process. On CI (and on developer machines where the user actually
uses mcp-agent-mail) that meant a test would:

  1. write implicit archive-mailbox files under the *real*
     `$XDG_DATA_HOME/mcp-agent-mail/git_mailbox_repo`,
  2. open a tempdir-scoped SQLite DB,
  3. read back messages and assert that the "direct SQLite" path
     saw what the DB had,

and flake whenever the implicit archive-mailbox path accidentally
agreed or disagreed with the DB for unrelated reasons.

Changes
-------
`crates/mcp-agent-mail-core/src/config.rs`:
  * Add a `TEST_SERIALIZER` `LazyLock<Mutex<()>>` and acquire it at
    the top of `with_process_env_overrides_for_test`. Process env
    is a global shared mutable, and `cargo test` runs tests in a
    thread pool by default; without the mutex, two tests racing
    through the override helper would save and restore each
    other's env values and reliably corrupt state.
  * Poison recovery uses `PoisonError::into_inner` so one panicking
    test does not permanently poison every subsequent test.

`crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/lib.rs`:
  * Wrap 30 test bodies in `with_process_env_overrides_for_test(
    &[("STORAGE_ROOT", &__storage_root_str)], || { ... })`, using
    the test's own `tempfile::tempdir()` path as the override
    value, so every implicit archive-mailbox layout the test
    creates lands inside the tempdir rather than under the real
    user data home.
  * Pairs with the core mutex: each test sees only its own
    `STORAGE_ROOT` for the duration of its closure, the override
    is restored on scope exit, and no two tests can be inside the
    helper at the same time.

No production code path changed. The override helper was already
gated behind `#[doc(hidden)]` and is a test-only entry point.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
